_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Hyperthermia with a core body temperature of at least 40.6°C (typically ranges from 40°C to 44°C)
Rectal temperature exceeds 40°C leading to a presumptive diagnosis of Heat stroke
Major illness characterized by altered consciousness, confusion or seizure in heatwave or high ambient temperature conditions
Patient present with confusion and agitation, ataxia and irritability

clinical_thresholds.note: for presumptive diagnosis of Heat stroke

preauth.label: Clinical notes with detailing history and Admission notes showing vitals (Temperature, BP, Pulse) and planned line of treatment
White Blood Count, Sr. electrolytes, Blood gas, Creatine phosphokinase, lactate dehydrogenase

claim.label: Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes with detailing history and Admission notes showing vitals (Temperature, BP, Pulse) Planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the White Blood Count, Sr. electrolytes, Blood gas, Creatine phosphokinase, lactate dehydrogenase report submitted?
Was the clinical notes and White Blood Count, Sr. electrolytes levels indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with treatment details submitted?
Was the Post treatment WBC, Sr. electrolytes submitted?
Was the Detailed Discharge Summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes, White Blood Count, or Sr. electrolytes levels are not indicative of Heat Stroke diagnosis/procedure.
Clinical notes, admission notes, vitals (Temperature, BP, Pulse), or planned line of treatment missing/incomplete at pre-authorization.
White Blood Count, Sr. electrolytes, Blood gas, Creatine phosphokinase, or lactate dehydrogenase report missing/incomplete at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with treatment details missing/incomplete at claim submission.
Post-treatment WBC or Sr. electrolytes report missing/incomplete at claim submission.
Detailed Discharge Summary missing/incomplete at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with management of Heat stroke only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs:
contraindications: Other causes of hyperpyrexia and altered consciousness (e.g. malaria, encephalitis, UTI, pneumonia, viral fever and neuroleptic malignant syndrome) should be excluded.
